recommendations.rationale: First-line and most sensitive investigation. Identifies intrauterine pregnancy (rules out ectopic in non-heterotopic cases), free fluid, adnexal mass, and cardiac activity. TVS is superior to transabdominal for early pregnancy.
recommendations.india_context: Available at all gynaecology units. Combined with serum beta-hCG for diagnosis and monitoring. All women of reproductive age with pelvic pain must have TVS. Informed consent required in unmarried women as per local practice.
recommendations.special_notes: Intrauterine gestational sac visible on TVS when serum hCG >1500–2000 IU/L (discriminatory zone). Adnexal ring sign or ectopic heartbeat is diagnostic.

recommendations.rationale: Useful initial scan when TVS not immediately available or patient declines TVS. Inferior to TVS for early intrauterine pregnancy detection.
recommendations.india_context: TAS widely available. In many Indian settings, TVS may require consent discussion. TAS can detect free fluid in haemoperitoneum and large adnexal masses.
recommendations.special_notes: Full bladder required for adequate TAS. If inconclusive, proceed to TVS.

recommendations.rationale: When TVS is inconclusive, especially for unusual locations (cornual, cervical, abdominal, scar ectopic). Avoids radiation — preferred over CT in pregnancy.
recommendations.india_context: Reserve for rare ectopic locations or when TVS is technically limited. Available at tertiary centres.
recommendations.special_notes: Avoid gadolinium in first trimester unless absolutely necessary.

recommendations.rationale: Generally avoided in suspected ectopic pregnancy due to radiation to early pregnancy/gonads. Only consider if ultrasound is unavailable and haemodynamic compromise requires rapid surgical planning.
recommendations.india_context: Avoid CT in reproductive-age women unless absolutely unavoidable. Ultrasound should be used in all cases.
recommendations.special_notes: If CT is unavoidable (e.g., haemoperitoneum, unstable, no US available), shield pelvic organs as much as possible.
contraindicated_if: known or suspected viable intrauterine pregnancy

scenarios.title: Acute Pelvic Pain / Suspected Ovarian Torsion
references: ACR AC Acute Pelvic Pain Female

recommendations.rationale: Essential first-line investigation. Assesses ovarian size, echogenicity, follicles, and Doppler flow. Absent or reduced blood flow is suspicious for torsion but normal flow does not exclude it.
recommendations.india_context: Available at all gynaecology units. TVS with colour Doppler is the investigation of choice. Torsion is a surgical emergency — image without delay.
recommendations.special_notes: Normal ovarian blood flow on Doppler does NOT exclude torsion (intermittent torsion, venous preservation). Clinical suspicion should guide decision to operate.

recommendations.rationale: Can identify enlarged ovary, adnexal mass, or free fluid. Less sensitive than TVS for early torsion.
recommendations.india_context: First-line where TVS is not immediately available or declined.

recommendations.rationale: High soft tissue resolution. Identifies twisted vascular pedicle (whirlpool sign), haemorrhagic infarction, and complex cysts. Use when US inconclusive and clinical suspicion persists.
recommendations.india_context: Use in pregnant patients with suspected torsion to avoid CT radiation.
recommendations.special_notes: T2-weighted sequences best for ovarian structure. Twisted pedicle seen as a hypointense band.

recommendations.rationale: When MRI unavailable and clinical suspicion high. Can detect twisted pedicle, enlarged heterogeneous ovary, and free fluid.
recommendations.india_context: Use only when US is inconclusive and MRI not available. Avoid in pregnancy or reproductive-age women unless essential.

recommendations.rationale: First-line for fibroid detection, number, size, location (FIGO classification), and endometrial assessment. Identifies submucosal fibroids best on TVS.
recommendations.india_context: Fibroids are among the most common indications for hysterectomy in India. TVS is universally available and first-line. Saline infusion sonography (SIS) for submucosal fibroids.
recommendations.special_notes: FIGO classification (Types 0–8) determines treatment. Submucosal (Types 0-2) cause most menorrhagia. Document number, size, and location of each fibroid.

recommendations.rationale: Gold standard for fibroid mapping before myomectomy or uterine artery embolisation (UAE). Identifies all fibroids including deep intramural, delineates relationship to endometrium and serosa.
recommendations.india_context: Mandatory before UAE or complex myomectomy. Differentiates fibroids from adenomyosis — both common in Indian women. Available at tertiary centres.
recommendations.special_notes: Distinguish leiomyoma (T2 hypointense) from adenomyosis (diffuse junctional zone thickening) — treatment differs. LGE post-UAE confirms infarction.

recommendations.rationale: Best for evaluation of submucosal fibroid component and endometrial cavity. Guides decision for hysteroscopic versus open myomectomy.
recommendations.india_context: Can be performed at gynaecology outpatient. Minimal equipment needed — saline, catheter, and TVS probe. Cost-effective.

recommendations.rationale: Poor soft tissue contrast for uterine assessment. Not recommended for routine fibroid evaluation. Use only if malignancy is suspected and MRI is unavailable.
recommendations.india_context: Avoid CT for benign fibroid evaluation — use MRI or ultrasound.

recommendations.rationale: First-line imaging. Identifies hydro/pyosalpinx, tubo-ovarian complex, tubo-ovarian abscess, free pelvic fluid. Essential to distinguish uncomplicated PID from TOA which requires different management.
recommendations.india_context: STI-related PID common in reproductive-age Indian women. TB-related PID/salpingitis also prevalent. Consider TB aetiology in chronic cases.
recommendations.special_notes: Cogwheel sign (incomplete septa in dilated tube) and beads-on-a-string sign are characteristic of hydrosalpinx. TOA appears as a complex adnexal mass.

recommendations.rationale: For suspected TOA with systemic sepsis, surgical planning, or to exclude other causes of acute abdomen. Identifies abscess location, extent, and complications.
recommendations.india_context: Use when clinical condition is severe or TOA does not respond to antibiotics. Required for surgical/radiological drainage planning.
recommendations.special_notes: Air within adnexal mass on CT = gas-forming abscess, surgical emergency.

recommendations.rationale: For complex cases where TVS is inconclusive or when TB salpingitis/endometritis needs to be excluded. Excellent soft tissue detail without radiation.
recommendations.india_context: In India, TB pelvic inflammatory disease is common. MRI helps distinguish TB from bacterial PID (TB shows calcifications, dense fibrosis, discrete lymph nodes).

recommendations.rationale: First-line for measuring endometrial thickness. Postmenopausal endometrial thickness >4 mm requires histological sampling. Identifies polyps, submucosal fibroids, and focal lesions.
recommendations.india_context: Endometrial carcinoma is the most common gynaecological malignancy in urban India. TVS is first-line and available everywhere. Doppler can identify vascular polyps.
recommendations.special_notes: Endometrial thickness: Premenopausal varies with cycle; Postmenopausal >4 mm warrants biopsy. Power Doppler helps identify pedunculated polyps.

recommendations.rationale: Gold standard for local staging of endometrial carcinoma (myometrial invasion depth, cervical extension). Essential for surgical planning and treatment decision.
recommendations.india_context: Stage IA vs IB (depth of myometrial invasion) determined by MRI dictates extent of lymph node dissection. Available at tertiary oncology centres.
recommendations.special_notes: FIGO staging: T2 sequences best for junctional zone integrity. DCE-MRI for myometrial invasion. Sagittal plane critical.

recommendations.rationale: Used for staging (lymph node assessment, distant metastasis). MRI superior for local staging but CT is standard for systemic staging.
recommendations.india_context: Combine with MRI pelvis for complete staging at oncology centres.
recommendations.special_notes: PET-CT increasingly used for nodal staging in high-grade tumours.

recommendations.rationale: Excellent for distinguishing endometrial polyp from focal hyperplasia. Best for pre-hysteroscopy assessment.
recommendations.india_context: Simple, cheap, and can be performed in outpatient clinic. Guides decision for hysteroscopic polypectomy.

recommendations.rationale: First-line investigation. IOTA (International Ovarian Tumour Analysis) criteria classify masses as benign or malignant. Features of malignancy: thick septae, solid components, papillary projections, ascites, bilateral.
recommendations.india_context: Epithelial ovarian cancer is most common in postmenopausal Indian women. Combine with serum CA-125. Early diagnosis significantly improves outcomes.
recommendations.special_notes: IOTA Simple Rules: M-features (malignant) vs B-features (benign). ADNEX model for risk stratification. Any solid component in postmenopausal ovarian cyst requires urgent further evaluation.

recommendations.rationale: Best for tissue characterisation of indeterminate adnexal masses on ultrasound. Identifies fat (dermoid), T2 dark fibromas, endometrioma, and enhancement patterns suggesting malignancy.
recommendations.india_context: Reserve for IOTA-indeterminate lesions. Available at oncology centres. Endometrioma (chocolate cyst) is very common in Indian women and has characteristic MRI appearance.
recommendations.special_notes: T1 fat-sat: endometrioma (T1 bright, T1 fat-sat bright = blood). T2: fibroma (T2 dark). LGE: malignant enhancement.

recommendations.rationale: Standard for ovarian cancer staging. Assesses peritoneal deposits, ascites, lymphadenopathy, liver and lung metastases. Guides surgical resectability assessment.
recommendations.india_context: Most ovarian cancers in India present at advanced stage (III/IV). CT chest/abdomen/pelvis essential for staging. PMJAY covers cancer staging investigations.
recommendations.special_notes: Omental caking, peritoneal deposits, ascites = advanced disease. Pre-op CT determines primary cytoreductive vs neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

recommendations.rationale: For suspected recurrence, restaging after chemotherapy, and evaluation of CA-125 rise with negative conventional imaging.
recommendations.india_context: Available at AIIMS, Tata Memorial, and major oncology centres. Increasingly covered under cancer treatment packages.
recommendations.special_notes: False positives possible in peritoneal deposits <1 cm. Combine with CA-125 trend for recurrence monitoring.
